blocks.text: DTC U129E: F-CAN communication error (PGM-FI ECU-Smart Power Control Unit communication failure)

Inter-control unit communication (F-CAN) for powertrain/chassis systems uses two communication lines (F-CAN_H and F-CAN_L) to send and receive multiple signals simultaneously between multiple control units. If signals from the MICU sent using F-CAN_H and F-CAN_L are not received for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
When the PGM-FI ECU cannot receive signals from the MICU sent using F-CAN_H and F-CAN_L for more than 1.5 seconds.

items.text: Disconnection of F-CAN circuit code (F-CAN_H line)
Disconnection of F-CAN circuit code (F-CAN_L line)
Ground fault in F-CAN circuit code (F-CAN_H line)
Ground fault in F-CAN circuit code (F-CAN_L line)

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory without the PGM-FI warning light being turned on.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
